Title: Pahang Rangers team manager, family hurt in head-on collision in Terengganu
Article:
KUANTAN: Pahang Rangers futsal team manager Muhammad Syahir Ab Aziz and his family were injured in a head-on collision at Setiu, Terengganu, on July 11.
Syahir, who is in his 30s, was travelling with his wife and their three children in a Honda HR-V sport utility vehicle when the accident occurred at 7.30pm.
A 40-second video of the crash, which has since gone viral, showed an oncoming Perodua Axia colliding head-on with the Honda during heavy rain. The condition of the Axia driver remains unknown.
Pahang Rangers coach Eloy Alonso said the incident happened while Syahir, who also serves as the club's sports director, was returning to Kuantan after attending the wedding of one of the team's players in Kelantan.
"Syahir and his family had attended the wedding of Pahang Rangers player Harith Naim (Mohd Nasir) and were on their way back to Kuantan. He was supposed to join the rest of the team in Shah Alam for our match yesterday.
"Our victory over the armed forces in the Malaysia Premier Futsal League yesterday is dedicated to Syahir and his family. We pray for their speedy recovery," he was quoted as saying by the Otai Station sports news portal today.
Syahir and his wife sustained minor injuries, while two of their children were admitted to Sultanah Nur Zahirah Hospital in Terengganu. The couple's eldest son escaped unhurt.
Pahang Rangers is a professional futsal club based in Kuantan that competes in the Malaysia Premier Futsal League.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia, South Korea launch AI academy to train digital talent
Article:
SHAH ALAM: The Ministry of Digital today launched the Korea-Asean Digital Academy (KADA), a joint initiative with the Republic of Korea to develop industry-ready artificial intelligence (AI) and digital professionals in Malaysia.
Coordinated by the Malaysia Digital Economy Corporation (MDEC), the programme will provide specialised training in AI and emerging digital technologies through four cohorts from 2026 to 2027, with a target of producing about 200 industry-ready Malaysian AI and digital professionals.
"Through the programme, participants will undergo specialised training in AI and emerging digital technologies, equipping them with practical skills, global perspectives and industry competencies to meet the evolving demands of the digital economy.
"Beyond talent development, KADA strengthens cooperation between Malaysia, the Republic of Korea and Asean member states by promoting knowledge exchange and advancing AI capability development across the region," MDEC said in a statement today.
The programme was launched by MDEC chief executive officer Anuar Fariz Fadzil, Republic of Korea Ambassador to Malaysia Yeo Seung-bae, National IT Industry Promotion Agency (NIPA) executive vice president Kim Deuk-jung, Universiti Teknologi MARA (UiTM) deputy vice-chancellor (Academic and International) Professor Dr Yamin Yasin, and UiTM Faculty of Computer and Mathematical Sciences dean Professor Dr Norjansalika Janom at UiTM.
Malaysia was selected as one of the participating countries under KADA following its endorsement at the 6th Asean Digital Ministers' Meeting (6th ADGMIN) in January, reflecting international confidence in the country's digital ecosystem, institutional capabilities and commitment to developing future-ready digital talent.
MDEC is implementing the programme in collaboration with strategic partners from the government, academia and industry.
UiTM, through its Faculty of Computer and Mathematical Sciences, was selected as Malaysia's host institution following an evaluation involving selected Premier Digital Tech Institutions.
The programme will be delivered by ABLEARN, NIPA's appointed service provider, in collaboration with Garranto Academy as the local operational partner.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Witness Protection (amendment) bill tabled for first reading
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Witness Protection (Amendment) Bill 2026, which aims to enhance witness protection and provide psychological support and post-protection assistance, was tabled for its first reading in the Dewan Rakyat today.
Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said, when tabling the Bill, said it would be presented for its second reading during the current sitting.
According to a copy of the Bill uploaded to Parliament's official portal, one of the key amendments would allow a parent or guardian to apply for admission into the witness protection programme on behalf of a witness who lacks legal capacity.
Clause 2 of the Bill seeks to amend subsection 7(3) of the Witness Protection Act 2009 (Act 696) by inserting the words "or lacks legal capacity" after the words "under the age of eighteen years."
Meanwhile, Clause 3 proposes the introduction of new Sections 10A and 10B into Act 696.
Under the proposed Section 10A, a participant in the witness protection programme, or a person acting on the participant's behalf, would be required to enter into a written agreement setting out the terms and conditions of the protection and assistance provided under the programme, the participant's obligations, and other related matters. The agreement must be executed before a designated officer.
The proposed Section 10B provides for the continuation of existing agreements entered into between programme participants and the government before the amendment comes into force, provided those agreements remain valid. It also stipulates that both parties will continue to be subject to, and entitled to, the rights and benefits provided under the amended Act.
Clause 4 seeks to amend subsection 13(2) of Act 696 to include psychological assistance, including counselling services, as part of the support that may be provided to participants in the witness protection programme.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Suspect nabbed after allegedly luring, molesting 10 young boys
Article:
MELAKA: A 36-year-old trader has been arrested for allegedly molesting 10 boys and sodomising one of them between March and this month.
The victims are aged between eight and 13.
State police chief Datuk Zulkhairi Mukhtar said the suspect was apprehended by members of the public at 6pm yesterday at the Taman Desa Duyong community hall before being handed over to the police.
He said initial investigations revealed the suspect was involved in 10 cases of physical sexual assault against 10 boys aged between eight and 13 from March to July this year.
The first case involves eight boys aged between eight and 12, believed to have occurred between May and early this month at a playground near Duyong.
"The suspect's modus operandi was to approach the victims at the playground or after they performed prayers at a surau before committing physical sexual assault against them," Zulkhairi said in a statement today.
He said the second case occurred in March, involving a 13-year-old boy who had known the suspect since last year through frequent meetings at a playground and a surau.
"The suspect allegedly took advantage of their relationship to commit physical sexual assault and carnal intercourse against the order of nature at his house in March.
"Investigations showed the suspect met the victim again on July 12," Zulkhairi said.
He added that the third case involved an 11-year-old boy who was molested several times on July 8 and 9 at Taman Desa Duyong, a supermarket, and an eatery in Semabok.
The suspect has been remanded for seven days until July 19 to assist with the investigation.
Zulkhairi said three investigation papers have been opened under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt to study plastics industry proposals as global supply crisis hits sector, says Akmal
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry (MITI) and the Economy Ministry have been directed to examine industry proposals submitted on issues facing the industry in the midst of the global supply crisis, said Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir.
He said the government was taking the issue seriously, with the National Economic Action Council (NEAC) noting several proposals on structural cost and competitiveness issues put forward by the Malaysian Plastics Manufacturers Association (MPMA) at NEAC's meeting today.
Akmal added that the government is directing both ministries to review them, taking into account the interests of the overall industry chain, fiscal balance, and the country's long-term economic competitiveness.
"Industry feedback shows that manufacturers are facing pressure on raw material costs, logistics, cash flow, and delivery timeline uncertainty.
"For some companies, the impact has already affected business contracts, investment decisions, and workforce management," he said in his briefing on the global supply crisis at Parliament
Akmal said industry feedback showed manufacturers were facing pressure on raw material costs, logistics, cash flow, and delivery timeline uncertainty, with some companies already seeing impacts on business contracts, investment decisions, and workforce management.
"MPMA also put forward views regarding several structural cost and competitiveness issues faced by downstream manufacturers, including differences in raw material costs compared with competing countries," he said.
Akmal said the plastics industry served as a critical support industry for many sectors, with its 2025 sales value amounting to RM62.69 billion, down from RM64.78 billion in 2024.
Packaging represented 45 per cent of the market, while electrical and electronics accounted for 29 per cent, he said.
He warned that pressure on the plastics industry could ripple through to food packaging, electrical and electronic components, automotive, medical devices, construction, agriculture, and manufacturing exports.
On Sunday, the number of vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz fell to multi-week lows, shipping data showed, as
renewed strikes
between the US and Iran and attacks on ships in the Middle East heightened safety concerns.
Six vessels transited the strait on Sunday, ship-tracking data from Kpler showed, the lowest number in five weeks.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fertiliser Bill to be tabled in early 2027 to strengthen supply resilience, says Akmal
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government is expected to table a Fertiliser Bill in early 2027 to strengthen governance, quality, and market transparency in the sector, as it moves to shore up the resilience of agricultural input supply amid the global supply crisis, said Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir.
He said the country's staple food supply remained stable and sufficient, with the rice buffer stock capable of meeting domestic needs for six months, and the price of Local White Rice maintained without any increase.
"The Fertiliser Bill is expected to be tabled in early 2027 to strengthen governance, quality, and market transparency, while enhancing the resilience of agricultural input supply," he said in his briefing on the global supply crisis at Parliament, following a meeting of the National Economic Action Council (NEAC).
Akmal said food security was the first matter discussed at today's NEAC meeting, alongside the pressure currently faced by the agricultural sector.
He said the government was taking seriously the risk of fertiliser price increases, with the Agriculture and Food Security Ministry diversifying input sources, strengthening assistance to farmers, and improving industry regulation.
"In an uncertain global environment, our priority is to ensure that basic supplies cannot be cut off and the people cannot be burdened with unreasonable price increases," he said.
Akmal said the government understood the pressure borne by padi farmers due to weather changes and rising production costs.
As at July 12, 2026, he said RM45.98 million had been disbursed through the Ploughing Incentive for Padi Farmers, benefiting 118,908 farmers in Peninsular Malaysia.
He added that NEAC had also taken note of the risks posed by hot weather and dam water levels, and that monitoring would be strengthened so that mitigation measures could be mobilised before production and supply to consumers were affected.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit closes higher against major, regional currencies
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit ended higher against major and regional currencies, including the US dollar, on Monday amid continued geopolitical uncertainties in Middle East.
At 6pm, the local currency edged up to 4.0680/0730 versus the greenback from 4.0695/0745 at last Friday's close.
Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said investors continued to assess uncertainties surrounding the United States-Iran ceasefire and the free flow of vessels through the Strait of Hormuz.
He said crude oil prices remained elevated, with West Texas Intermediate and Brent crude trading at US$73.02 and US$77.80 per barrel, respectively.
"Meanwhile, US interest-rate futures indicated a 55.7 per cent probability of a quarter-point increase in the federal funds rate in September.
"As such, this is expected to continue providing support for the US dollar in the near term," he told Bernama.
At the close, the ringgit traded higher against a basket of major currencies.
It appreciated versus the Japanese yen to 2.5110/5142 from 2.5148/5181 at last Friday's close, strengthened against the British pound to 5.4475/4542 from 5.4572/4639 at the end of last week, and inched up vis-a-vis the euro to 4.6501/6558 from 4.6502/6559 previously.
The ringgit also performed better against regional currencies.
It rose versus the Singapore dollar to 3.1489/1530 from 3.1520/1561 at the previous close and strengthened against the Indonesian rupiah to 224.6/225.0 from 225.2/225.6.
The local currency was also firmer against the Thai baht to 12.1953/2151 from 12.2101/2302 previously and gained vis-a-vis the Philippine peso to 6.60/6.62 from 6.61/6.62.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Geopolitics key to sukuk outlook, but 2026 issuance seen lower: Fitch
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Global sukuk issuance in 2026 will hinge on whether the US-Iran ceasefire holds and leads to broader stability in the Middle East, according to Fitch Ratings.
A more stable geopolitical environment could improve funding conditions, while renewed tensions may dampen economic growth and issuance activity.
Market direction is expected to become clearer after the summer holiday period, when issuers finalise funding plans and investors adjust their portfolios.
Fitch expects global sukuk issuance in 2026 to remain below 2025 levels.
"We expect global sukuk issuance in 2026 to remain below 2025 levels, with recovery dependent on whether recent geopolitical easing translates into more durable regional stability," said Bashar Al Natoor, global head of Islamic finance at Fitch Ratings.
He said credit fundamentals remain resilient, with no sukuk defaults since 2021 and more than 80 per cent of Fitch-rated sukuk carrying investment-grade ratings.
However, the proportion of issuers with stable outlooks slipped to 80 per cent in the first half of 2026.
After slowing in March and April, sukuk issuance resumed across both investment-grade and speculative-grade borrowers.
Still, higher market volatility and rising yields caused sukuk issuance across the GCC, Malaysia, Indonesia, Turkiye and Pakistan to fall 36 per cent year-on-year to US$125 billion in the first half of 2026.
By comparison, bond issuance edged down just 0.8 per cent.
Despite the slowdown in new issuance, global sukuk outstanding grew 11 per cent to US$1.1 trillion.
Sukuk accounted for nine per cent of US dollar debt issued in emerging markets, excluding China, during the period.
GCC investment-grade sukuk also continued to offer lower yields than conventional bonds.
Saudi Arabia is unlikely to issue new public US dollar sovereign sukuk in the second half of 2026 after completing its funding programme.
Indonesia's issuance is expected to remain strong as it continues to meet financing needs, while the UAE is likely to rely on sukuk as government debt rises to an estimated 25 per cent of GDP in 2026.
In Malaysia, the government's target to reduce federal debt to 60 per cent of GDP or below by 2030 could limit sovereign sukuk issuance.
However, banks and corporates are expected to continue tapping the sukuk market when funding conditions are favourable.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Wyndham, 1001 Nights showcase at Everse
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Wyndham Ion Majestic Hotel and 1001 Nights Hotel, part of NCT Group, will showcase their hospitality and tourism offerings at Everse 2026, a youth-focused event to be held at Hextar World, Empire City, from July 18-19.
The participation forms part of the two-day event organised in partnership with SEGi University College, which features esports, K-pop, cosplay, entrepreneurship and content creation activities.
Visitors to the hotels' booth will be introduced to attractions and facilities at the Grand Ion Majestic integrated development in Genting Highlands through interactive activities, including themed photo opportunities, travel quizzes and games.
The hotels will also offer an exclusive accommodation package during the event.
Executive vice president of Ion Majestic Hospitality Sdn Bhd and NCT Group corporate strategy and business development Tee Yih Fung said hospitality is about creating memorable moments that connect us.
"At Everse, we will give visitors a glimpse of 1001 Nights Hotel and Wyndham Ion Majestic Hotel and why they are a must-visit destination in Genting Highlands.
"Everse celebrates creativity, connection and discovery, and these values resonate with what we deliver every day through meaningful guest experiences," he said.
Located within NCT Group's flagship Grand Ion Majestic integrated development in Genting Highlands, 1001 Nights Hotel and Wyndham Ion Majestic Hotel sits approximately 6,000 feet above sea level, combining hospitality, leisure and lifestyle in a tranquil mountain setting.
The hotels feature panoramic highland views and signature attractions including Malaysia's highest rooftop sky pyramid, heated swimming pool, a glass-bottom link bridge, and dedicated event spaces, creating a contemporary escape amid the cool mountain climate.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Migrant worker rights advocate accused of threatening Bangladesh journalists
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: British migrant worker rights advocate Andy Hall has been accused by a Bangladeshi news outlet of threatening journalists and making racist remarks following media coverage of his recent visit to Bangladesh, although the allegations have not been independently verified.
According to Bangladesh-based Daily Tribunal 24, the controversy followed Hall's visit to Dhaka from July 1-5, during which he met government officials and stakeholders to discuss safe migration, ethical recruitment, migrant workers' rights and the protection of Bangladeshi workers at home and abroad.
The publication reported that Hall also held discussions with senior Bangladeshi officials on matters related to the Malaysia-Bangladesh labour migration corridor, as both countries work towards reopening Malaysia's labour market to Bangladeshi workers.
Daily Tribunal 24 said Hall met Bangladesh's Expatriates' Welfare and Overseas Employment Minister, the prime minister's special assistant, recruitment agency representatives and non-governmental organisation officials.
During the meetings, the report alleged that Hall raised concerns over aspects of the Foreign Workers Centralised Management System (FWCMS), which is used in Malaysia's foreign worker recruitment process.
The publication further alleged that Bangladeshi officials rejected Hall's proposals and maintained their support for reopening the Malaysian labour market, after which discussions became heated.
Following media reports on his visit, Daily Tribunal 24 alleged that Hall sent WhatsApp messages to journalists and media executives threatening legal action over their coverage.
The publication also reported that Hall accused local media organisations of defamation while indicating that lawyers were preparing legal action.
Hall, a long-time labour rights campaigner, has been active in advocating for migrant worker rights across Southeast Asia, including Malaysia.
He has worked with civil society organisations on issues related to recruitment practices, forced labour and migrant worker welfare, and has frequently been quoted by international media.
At the time Daily Tribunal 24 published its report, it did not include Hall's response to the specific allegations.
Separately, Bangladeshi news portal The Finance Today on July 9 published an apology to Hall and retracted an earlier report titled "Malaysia Labour Market: Controversy erupts over Andy Hall's Bangladesh visit" following a formal protest from him.
In its apology, editor and publisher Md. Motiur Rahman acknowledged that the publication had relied on "false allegations and wrong intelligence" regarding Hall's alleged misconduct involving Bangladesh's Expatriates' Welfare and Overseas Employment Ministry.
"We deeply regret any harm, reputational damage or distress this oversight has caused you. We take full responsibility for this error," the publication said.
The Finance Today also published Hall's full statement "to set the record straight."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Undercover officers, unmarked vehicles deployed in Pahang immigration raids
Article:
KUANTAN: The Immigration Department has deployed undercover officers to track down undocumented foreigners after previous operations were compromised due to the suspected involvement of 'tonto'(lookouts).
Plain-clothes officers, some disguised as air-conditioning technicians, individuals wearing baju Melayu with kopiah, and others in formal office attire, are able to mix more effectively with the public while gathering intelligence and carrying out enforcement operations.
The department has also deployed unmarked vehicles to enable officers to blend in with civilian traffic and avoid exposing their identities before arriving for raids.
Pahang Immigration Department director Nursafariza Ihsan said that, as many undocumented foreigners had become familiar with the department's officers and official vehicles, new methods were needed to gather intelligence before launching special operations.
"Yes, tontos are involved....information could be provided either by those (Malaysians) who hire the foreigners or by the large groups of foreigners themselves working closely with third parties, who may be paid to pass on information.
"Several employers remain undeterred by previous penalties and the arrest of their workers for immigration offences. Our inspections repeatedly uncover the same employers continuing to hire undocumented immigrants or misuse work passes," she told reporters after a special operation here today.
Nursafariza said some employers continued to hire foreigners who abused their immigration passes by violating the conditions attached to them.
"These employers had previously been penalised for immigration offences but continue to employ foreigners who misuse the passes granted to them by taking up jobs that are inconsistent with the original purpose of their passes," she said.
Meanwhile, on the department's large-scale special operation conducted here today, Nursafariza said the enforcement team screened 183 people at 19 premises and arrested 32 foreigners.
"A total of 27 men and five women, aged between 21 and 55, from Indonesia, Myanmar, India, Pakistan and Bangladesh were arrested.
"The offences included overstaying, failing to possess valid identification documents, and abusing their work passes. They will be sent to the Kemayan Immigration Depot in Bera," she said.
She said that the department had screened 4,227 foreigners and arrested 1,157 individuals for various immigration offences across 11 districts in Pahang between Jan 1 and today.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Psychologist says teen's diary entries were a temporary expression of anger
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The late Zara Qairina Mahathir's angry expressions in her diaries were temporary and should not be taken as a reflection of her overall relationship with her mother, the coroner's court heard today.
Child psychologist Dr Noor Aishah Rosli, 51, testified that when children or teenagers face problems, some choose to express their emotions by writing in diaries.
She said studies have shown that diaries are often perceived as a safe space for expression because the writer assumes no one else will read them.
"For this case, Zara was writing about her unhappiness only at that time," Noor Aishah said.
"When she was writing at the age of 11 or 12, she could not regulate her emotions during that period. Maybe something had happened at home at that time.
"I believe if someone has the opportunity to look back (at the writing), maybe those emotions would be gone, and she would even wonder, 'Why did I write this way?' she added.
She told coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan that the content of the diaries could not be used as a determinant of Zara's relationship with her mother because many other factors had to be considered.
Noor Aishah was the 76th witness testifying on the 79th day of the inquest into the 13-year-old's death last year.
In April, consultant forensic psychiatrist Dr Chua Sze Hung had testified that Zara Qairina's
diaries expressed anger and disappointment
, including expletives directed at her mother, which revealed the genuine psychological pain the teenager had endured.
When lawyer Shahlan Jufri examined whether Zara Qairina's use of foul language against her mother, Noraidah Lamat, reflected their relationship, Noor Aishah said it was merely a temporary expression of resentment.
She noted that the pair continued to spend quality time together and had positive conversations up to July last year.
The teenager was found unconscious on July 16 last year on the ground floor of her school dormitory at SKM Agama Tun Datu Mustapha. She died the following day.
The expert witness also said Zara Qairina was neither depressed nor experiencing hallucinations despite the alleged presence of barcode injuries on her body.
When Shahlan, who represents Noraidah, asked how a person is diagnosed with depression, Noor Aishah said the assessment refers to the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders.
She said symptoms assessed include persistent low mood, crying without reason, loss of interest, and deterioration in academic performance, which must persist over a sustained period before a diagnosis can be made.
"If we look at it... Zara did not have depression," she said.
The inquest will resume tomorrow.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan polls: DAP faces battle against negative perception created by rivals
Article:
SEREMBAN: DAP claims it will face a severe challenge following attempts by rival parties to play up racial narratives throughout the campaign period for the 16th Negri Sembilan election.
Its secretary-general, Anthony Loke, said DAP knows its rivals would put the blame for issues in Negri Sembilan on it.
"We know that the opposition has no other ammunition except to blame DAP for whatever issue happens in Negri Sembilan as their most effective capital.
"That is the narrative and strategy they are employing because they want to force our component partners in Pakatan Harapan into a defensive position.
"Everything is DAP's fault, and DAP must be rejected. We see Perikatan Nasional, especially Pas, has already launched this kind of attack, and they (Pas) want us to lose even though they themselves cannot win," he told reporters after launching the PH operations room and announcing the coalition's new candidate for the Temiang seat.
Also present was deputy state DAP chairman Datuk P. Gunasekaran and the party's state secretary Cha Kee Chin.
Loke said he observed that this strategy had been employed by the opposition in Johor, declining to field candidates in several seats and instead supported Barisan Nasional candidates with the sole purpose of defeating the PH candidates.
"This was openly admitted by a veteran leader who is now with Pas. However, we will explain to the public and PH supporters the vital importance of them turning out in droves on polling day," he said.
On a related matter, Loke urged BN component parties, specifically MCA and MIC, to state their stances following Pas' move to cooperate with BN and support Umno's candidate for menteri besar should they win the Negeri Sembilan election on Aug 1.
"At the federal level, MCA and MIC are members of the unity government, and they know that non-Malay worry about and are not comfortable with Pas, so if there is cooperation, everything should be out in the open," he said.
Loke said the MCA and MIC leadership should enter the Negri Sembilan election arena with a clear stance, rather than what "hiding things", which he claimed was what happened during the Johor election.
"In Johor, Pas provided support and assisted (BN) candidates, including those from MCA and MIC, behind the scenes but didn't dare to admit it. MCA and MIC themselves did not admit to any cooperation with Pas.
"Don't hide, don't do things in secrecy; if the people accept it, that is their right," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Volkswagen chief says 50,000 more job cuts may be needed to close competitive gap
Article:
BERLIN: Volkswagen may need to cut about 50,000 more jobs to match the competitiveness of rivals, its chief executive told staff in an internal memo, effectively confirming for the first time that the automaker is looking to reduce up to 100,000 positions.
Oliver Blume is battling to streamline Europe's largest carmaker, whose profits have slumped as it faces billions of dollars in tariff costs, fierce competition in China and pressure on its German manufacturing network to improve efficiency.
After already agreeing to 50,000 job cuts across the group, including its Porsche and Audi subsidiaries, the company must further reduce costs after calculating a 20 per cent cost disadvantage compared with comparable companies, Blume said in the memo seen by Reuters.
This translates into a "theoretical deduction" of another 50,000 jobs worldwide, the memo said.
"We are currently assessing across all brands, companies and regions how many adjustments are actually necessary and feasible," Blume said in the document.
The company had previously declined to comment on reports that it was considering up to 100,000 job losses.
The memo follows angry calls from workers for management to explain its restructuring plans, which Blume presented to the company's supervisory board on Thursday.
Sources familiar with the matter said labour representatives on the committee blocked the proposals, which were understood to include job cuts and the possible closure of four factories.
"As of today, we still cannot confirm competitive use cases for the plants of Emden, Hanover, Zwickau and Neckarsulm in the 2030s," Blume said in the memo.
He said he preferred "intelligent solutions" to closures, having previously pointed to the defence industry or the production of Chinese Volkswagen models in Europe as options for underutilised factories.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DAP names Loke's aide Ho Weng Wah for Temiang seat
Article:
SEREMBAN: DAP has named Transport Minister Anthony Loke's political secretary, Ho Weng Wah, as its candidate for the Temiang state seat in the 16th Negri Sembilan election, replacing incumbent Ng Chin Tsai.
Loke, who is DAP secretary-general, said Ho is one of three new faces fielded by the party for the polls, alongside Lee Kai Yet in Mambau and S. Mugunthan in Seremban Jaya.
The remaining eight incumbent assemblymen will defend their seats.
Loke said the party had conducted a comprehensive assessment ahead of the election, including identifying potential new candidates as part of its efforts to rejuvenate the party.
"However, for this election, we have adopted a more cautious approach by retaining most of our incumbents, who have strong service records and are well known to the voters," he told reporters after announcing Ho as the Temiang candidate here today.
"This is a critical election. We cannot afford to take risks in any constituency," he said.
Loke said Ho, 42, a Temiang local, had served as his aide since 2000 before being appointed his political secretary at the Transport Ministry in 2022.
"He is a responsible individual with credibility and integrity. He has managed all matters relating to constituency allocations for my Seremban parliamentary constituency efficiently, transparently, and without any issues," Loke said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Plantation, commodity stocks lift Bursa Malaysia higher at close
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended higher on Monday as gains in plantation and commodity-related counters offset the cautious regional sentiment.
At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose 6.95 points, or 0.41 per cent, to 1,698.44 from Friday's close of 1,691.49.
The benchmark index opened 1.81 points higher at 1,693.30, and moved between 1,685.74 and 1,703.10 throughout the day. The index briefly breached the key 1,700-level in the late afternoon trading session.
On the broader market, losers led gainers 653 to 440, while 533 counters were unchanged, 1,088 untraded, and 33 suspended.
Turnover improved to 3.09 billion units valued at RM2.23 billion from 2.85 billion units valued at RM2.25 billion on Friday.
Rakuten Trade Sdn Bhd vice-president of Equity Research Thong Pak Leng remains cautiously optimistic despite the elevated geopolitical risks.
He said strength in commodity-related sectors and resilient domestic market fundamentals are expected to lend support to the local bourse.
"We anticipate the benchmark index to trend within the 1,690-1,710 range for the week," he told Bernama.
IPPFA Sdn Bhd director of investment strategy and country economist Mohd Sedek Jantan said although the benchmark index finished just below the 1,700-point psychological level, the market remained resilient, supported by the return of foreign investors, who were net buyers over the past three trading sessions, providing renewed liquidity and support for large-cap counters.
"Regionally, Asian equities closed mixed as investors balanced geopolitical risks against improving fundamentals in selected markets, while Malaysia's stable macroeconomic outlook and sustained foreign interest continued to support domestic equities," he added.
Among heavyweights, Maybank rose four sen to RM10.94, Public Bank and IHH Healthcare added three sen to RM4.92 and RM8.47, respectively, CIMB gained five sen to RM7.69, while Tenaga Nasional was flat at RM14.30.
As for the active stocks, Tanco inched up 4.5 sen to 24 sen, Zetrix AI and VS Industry increased 1.5 sen each to 76.5 sen and 21 sen, respectively. HHRG put on half a sen to 11.5 sen while PUC was flat at 4.5 sen.
Of the top gainers, United Plantations garnered 76 sen to RM35.02, Kuala Lumpur Kepong was up 28 sen to RM21.88, SD Guthrie added 21 sen to RM6.45, while Press Metal and Sarawak Oil Palms climbed 15 sen each to RM7.85 and RM5.15, respectively.
Malaysian Pacific Industries led the losers list, falling RM1.70 to RM44.30, Fraser & Neave dipped 76 sen to RM28.94, Petronas Dagangan lost 34 sen to RM19.22, UWC slipped 29 sen to RM6.01, while Kelington erased 26 sen to RM7.70.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Congo's Ebola outbreak spreads to two more provinces
Article:
DAKAR: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo has spread to two more northeastern provinces, Haut-Uele and Tshopo, the country's public health institute said in its latest report.
The number of confirmed Ebola cases across the country rose to 1,926, including 702 deaths, official data showed late on Sunday.
Four cases were recorded in Tshopo, including two deaths, with one death confirmed in Haut-Uele, as of Saturday.
The latest Ebola outbreak, Congo's 17th, was declared on May 15 and has been largely concentrated in Ituri province, with cases also reported in North Kivu and South Kivu provinces.
The often fatal viral disease spreads through direct contact with bodily fluids from infected people or animals and causes symptoms that can include high fever, vomiting and internal ⁠and external bleeding.
Reuters reported in late June that Congolese health authorities had started tracing people potentially exposed to Ebola in Tshopo and Haut-Uele, but until now the two provinces had not been included in the government's daily reports.
"Although current investigations suggest that all cases detected in these two provinces are primarily imported from Niania in Ituri, it is necessary and appropriate ... to consider these two provinces as an epidemic zone," the National Institute of Public Health said in its report dated July 11.
Tshopo's provincial capital is Kisangani, one of Congo's largest cities. Haut-Uele shares borders with South Sudan and the Central African Republic.
A senior World Health Organisation official told Reuters last week that the true scale of the outbreak could be two to four times larger than official data indicate because four out of five new Ebola cases have no known link to existing patients.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia can be global stingless bee honey market
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia has the potential to become a global hub for stingless bee honey as the industry moves towards international quality standards, scientific validation and wider commercial applications, according to Stingless Bee Factor Pte Ltd (SBF).
For years, SBF said, the growing global interest in stingless bee honey has been held back by the lack of a common definition, recognised quality benchmarks and certification framework.
The absence of such standards has affected consumer confidence and limited opportunities for international trade.
To address these gaps, SBF launched the Future of Honey 2026 here, introducing what it described as the world's first international certification framework for stingless bee honey.
The framework aims to establish clearer standards for quality, authenticity and transparency, while supporting further development of the industry across agriculture, research and exports.
The launch also highlighted scientific studies examining the nutritional and functional properties of stingless bee honey.
Researchers from the Glycaemic Index Research Unit at Temasek Polytechnic, Singapore, found that certified stingless bee honey demonstrated a low glycaemic index (GI), providing scientific evidence on its potential as a dietary option for consumers concerned about blood sugar management.
Additional research by Temasek Polytechnic's School of Applied Science identified antibacterial and antioxidant properties in stingless bee honey, supporting further exploration of its potential applications in functional foods and nutraceutical products.
SBF said the industry is increasingly looking beyond honey as a conventional food product, with growing interest in natural ingredients linked to health and wellness.
SBF founder Javier Lau said the company has spent more than a decade working with researchers and institutions to develop standards that could help position stingless bee honey as a globally recognised product.
Potential future applications include nutritional supplements, wellness products and other health-related uses, although further research will be needed to establish broader commercial applications.
Malaysia's tropical environment, rich biodiversity and variety of stingless bee species provide a foundation for industry growth.
The introduction of international certification standards could help improve market transparency, strengthen consumer trust and create opportunities for local beekeepers, researchers and exporters.
The launch in Malaysia also reflects the country's ambition to play a larger role in the global stingless bee honey ecosystem.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Six Myanmar nationals, including two children, held in immigration raid
Article:
ALOR GAJAH: Two young boys were among six Myanmar nationals detained by the Melaka Immigration Department for operating a grocery shop selling imported goods and alcohol during a raid at Taman Mewah, Kelemak, today.
State Immigration director Anirwan Fauzee Mohd Aini said the 10am raid saw the detention of four adults — three women and a man in their 40s — who only possessed passports. At the same time, the two boys, aged one and seven, had no valid travel or identity documents.
He said preliminary investigations revealed that the premises, which were equipped with closed-circuit television cameras inside and outside, were believed to have been operating for two years.
"Based on preliminary information obtained from the Myanmar nationals, the premises are owned by a local individual and the business licence is believed to have been involved in an Ali Baba arrangement, whereby a local licence holder fronts a business effectively operated by foreigners," he said.
Anirwan Fauzee added that the operation targeted several premises in the Kelemak area believed to be employing or operated by foreign nationals.
In the same operation, three Indian men working at a mamak restaurant in Taman Seri Kelemak were detained for overstaying.
The three men, aged in their 30s, were working as waiters, roti canai makers, and beverage preparers when the raid was conducted.
"Inspection of the restaurant also uncovered a room suspected of being used for online gambling top-up activities," Anirwan Fauzee said.
An Indonesian woman in her late 20s was detained at the premises for not possessing valid travel documents and is suspected of working as a salesperson for the online gambling service.
A total of 64 foreigners were checked during the operation carried out across the Alor Gajah and Melaka Tengah districts, with 40 detained for various immigration offences, including lacking valid identification documents, breaching pass conditions, and overstaying.
Those detained comprised 27 men, including the two children, and 13 women, consisting of seven Indonesians, seven Bangladeshis, 14 Indians, eight Myanmar nationals, and four Nepalese, aged between one and 40.
Several foreigners attempted to flee into a nearby forest and crossed the road recklessly during the operation, but all were successfully apprehended.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ukrainian drones hit 15 more Russian vessels in Sea of Azov
Article:
KYIV: Ukrainian drones hit 15 Russian vessels in the Sea of Azov overnight, including seven tankers, Ukraine's drone forces commander said on Monday.
That brings the total number of vessels struck in the past eight days to 105, Robert Brovdi said on the Telegram messaging app.
In an earlier report, a Ukrainian drone struck a tanker as it was entering the Azov-Black Sea Canal, Yury Slyusar, governor of Russia's Rostov region, said on Sunday.
The fire caused by the attack has been brought under control and there was no risk of an oil spill because the vessel was empty, Slyusar said on Telegram, adding that there were no casualties.
The Ukrainian military has recently attacked more than 40 Russian tankers in the Sea of Azov as part of what Ukraine describes as a campaign aimed at disrupting fuel supplies to Russian forces and isolating Moscow-occupied Crimea.
Ukraine has intensified attacks on logistics and energy infrastructure in Crimea in recent weeks, contributing to fuel shortages and prompting authorities to declare a state of emergency in the peninsula.
On Saturday,  President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Ukraine is setting up a "long-range impact" command within its armed forces as Kyiv's campaign against Russian energy and logistics has forced Moscow to ban diesel exports and restrict shipping near the Sea of Azov, which abuts the Black Sea.
For months, Ukrainian attack drones have been targeting key energy infrastructure thousands of kilometres across Russia in what Kyiv casts as long-range sanctions against the primary contributor to Russia's state budget, backing its war effort.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Influencer arrested for allegedly raping, taking lewd pictures of 14-year-old girl
Article:
KANGAR: An influencer has been arrested for allegedly raping and taking lewd photographs of a 14-year-old girl at a rented house in Taman Kangar here yesterday.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Yusharifuddin Mohd Yusop said the 23-year-old suspect was arrested after the victim lodged a police report yesterday afternoon.
He said initial investigations revealed the incident began at 12.01am on June 17 when the suspect picked up the victim from her home in Kampung Wang Kelian, Kaki Bukit in a Proton Saga.
The suspect took the victim for dinner at a restaurant in Bintong before bringing her to his rented house in Taman Kangar.
"At 3.10am, the suspect asked the victim to enter the house and allegedly raped her in a bedroom, while also recording photographs of her during the incident," Yusharifuddin said in a statement today.
He added that the suspect then sent the victim back to her home at 4am on the same day.
Yusharifuddin said the suspect, who works as a shoe shop assistant in Kuala Lumpur, has been remanded for seven days until July 19 to assist with the investigation.
The remand order was issued by magistrate Ana Rozana Mohd Nor this morning.
The victim has been taken to Tuanku Fauziah Hospital in Kangar for a medical examination.
Yusharifuddin said the case is being investigated under Section 376 of the Penal Code for rape and Section 5 of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar pays courtesy call on Singaporean president during state visit
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today paid a courtesy call on Singaporean President Tharman Shanmugaratnam in conjunction with the latter's three-day state visit to Malaysia.
The 40-minute meeting here was also attended by Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan, Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani and Entrepreneur and Cooperatives Development Minister Steven Sim, as well as senior officials from both countries.
Earlier, Tharman was accorded a state welcoming ceremony at Istana Negara by the His Majesty, Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia.
The visit, from July 13 to 15 at the invitation of the King, reciprocates His Majesty's state visit to Singapore from May 6 to 7, 2024.
Singapore remains Malaysia's second-largest trading partner globally. Regionally, Singapore is Malaysia's largest trading partner among ASEAN member states.
Total bilateral trade between the two countries reached RM402.35 billion in 2025, an increase of 1.5 per cent from 2024.
From January to May this year, bilateral trade rose 14.5 per cent to RM189.90 billion, compared with RM165.93 billion recorded during the corresponding period in 2025.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hamas rejects top UN official accusation of Gaza aid obstruction
Article:
TEL AVIV: Hamas rejected an accusation from a senior UN official on Monday of interfering with humanitarian deliveries in Gaza and intimidating aid workers.
An official from the Hamas-run interior ministry in Gaza called the accusation unfounded.
"These are baseless accusations. The police and security forces continue to protect aid trucks and distribution centres and facilitate the work of international and humanitarian organisations," he told AFP.
He said "the police will not allow any attacks on humanitarian workers."
In a statement earlier, UN Deputy Special Coordinator for the Middle East Peace Process, Ramiz Alakbarov, said he "strongly" condemned the obstruction of humanitarian operations by Gaza's de facto authorities, referring to Hamas.
Hamas's actions "endangered humanitarian personnel, intimidated workers delivering life-saving food assistance and disrupted life-saving humanitarian operations", he said.
Armed men linked to Hamas allegedly forced their way into a food distribution point in Jabalia, in the northern Gaza Strip on Saturday, the UN statement said.
Fighters "also entered a WFP (World Food Programme) warehouse and reportedly assaulted two truck drivers who were delivering humanitarian supplies", it added.
Alakbarov said "these incidents are not isolated" and "reflect an increasingly dangerous pattern of intimidation, violence and obstruction, including smuggling attempts, targeting and abusing humanitarian operations."
He warned that such actions were hampering the delivery of life-saving assistance at a time when civilians across Gaza faced severe hardships.
A ceasefire was reached in Gaza between Israel and Hamas in October following two years of war, which was sparked by the Palestinian fighters' attack on Israel on Oct 7, 2023.
The second phase of the ceasefire, which was to involve Hamas's disarmament and a gradual withdrawal of Israeli forces from Gaza, has been stalled for months.
Since the ceasefire, Israeli forces have expanded their presence in recent months, taking control of more than 60 per cent of the territory.
Hamas still exerts control over the remaining area, but last week announced it was dissolving its 15-member body that had governed the strip for nearly two decades.
Violence continues in Gaza despite the ceasefire.
At least 1,098 Palestinians have been killed since the truce took effect, according to the territory's health ministry, whose figures are considered reliable by the United Nations.
The Israeli military says it has lost five soldiers in Gaza over the same period, as well as one civilian contractor.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MACC proposes 6 measures to tighten temple, church grant management
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has proposed six governance improvements to strengthen the management and disbursement of maintenance grants for non-Muslim houses of worship nationwide.
The recommendations, submitted by the Negri Sembilan MACC to the Housing and Local Government Ministry, followed a governance review after investigations uncovered cases where recipients failed to carry out approved maintenance works despite receiving government funding.
Among the key proposals is requiring all non-Muslim houses of worship applying for grants to be registered with the Registrar of Societies (ROS) or recognised under relevant religious laws.
MACC also recommended stricter controls on extension of time (EOT) approvals by limiting them to unforeseen circumstances, as well as introducing a procedure to recover unused funds or grants from recipients who breach application conditions.
To improve monitoring, the commission proposed a more detailed repair works form and a standardised physical inspection report by technical teams, supported by photographs taken during site visits.
The sixth recommendation calls for applicants to submit a declaration under Section 18 of the MACC Act 2009, certifying that all documents and information provided are accurate and comply with existing regulations.
The proposals were presented to Deputy Housing and Community Wellbeing Secretary-General Zulkeflee Sulaiman, who represented ministry secretary-general Datuk Dr M Noor Azman Taib.
Zulkeflee welcomed the recommendations and agreed to adopt them as part of efforts to strengthen integrity, governance and internal controls in the grant management system.
He also signed the Governance Improvement Report during the presentation ceremony, witnessed by Negri Sembilan MACC director Awgkok Ahmad Taufik Putra Awg Ismail.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tune Talk expands focus from mobile services to digital ecosystems
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR
: As Malaysia's telecommunications industry matures and mobile data becomes increasingly abundant, consumer expectations are evolving beyond traditional considerations such as data quotas and call plans.
This was among the key topics discussed during a recent
Beyond the Headlines
special, featuring Tune Talk executive director and chief executive officer Gurtaj Singh Padda, who shared the company's vision behind its newly launched brand direction, "Kasi Tune".
The discussion centred on how telecommunications providers are adapting to changing consumer behaviours in an increasingly digital-first environment, where users are seeking greater convenience, flexibility and value from their service providers.
According to Gurtaj, Tune Talk's latest positioning reflects a broader ambition that extends beyond connectivity alone. Rather than focusing solely on mobile plans, the company aims to create digital experiences that are more relevant to consumers' daily lives.
Industry observers note that competition among telcos has become increasingly challenging as many providers now offer similar core services, including generous data allocations, unlimited calls, 5G access and app-based rewards programmes.
As a result, differentiation is increasingly being driven by ecosystem offerings, personalised experiences, entertainment services and lifestyle integrations.
REDEFINING CUSTOMER LOYALTY
During the conversation, attention was also given to changing perceptions of customer loyalty. While pricing remains an important consideration, convenience and seamless user experiences are becoming equally influential factors in shaping consumer preferences.
This has prompted telecommunications providers to rethink their role, moving beyond network connectivity and towards becoming broader digital service enablers. And this applies to Tune Talk as well, offering extra benefits for their users.
"The idea is that when you say 'Tune Up Your World', that means if you have a plan in Malaysia or going to Hajj or Umrah, you carry your 500GB, your value-added services and just about everything with you for Hajj or Umrah," Gurtaj shared.
Another major topic explored was consumer fatigue surrounding subscription services and complex pricing structures.
Gurtaj acknowledged that many consumers today are not necessarily frustrated by cost alone, but by a lack of clarity regarding what they are paying for. Hidden charges, recurring subscriptions, promotional pricing structures and various add-ons can often create confusion and erode trust over time.
"Malaysians love transparency. So, under the umbrella of "Kasi Tune", let's make everything transparent. There should be no hidden FUP (fair usage policies)," he said.
Within the prepaid segment in particular, users tend to place a high value on spending control, budgeting flexibility and pricing transparency.
The conversation suggested that simplicity and predictability are increasingly becoming competitive advantages, with transparency itself emerging as an important component of the overall customer experience.
POWERED BY DIGITAL INFRASTRUCTURE
The discussion also touched on Tune Talk's "Cloud Powered" approach.
"We are at the bleeding edge, we are the first ones in Asia to put everything on the cloud," Gurtaj shared, highlighting the growing importance of cloud-native infrastructure in delivering real-time, personalised digital experiences.
As consumer expectations continue to evolve, technology platforms capable of supporting rapid innovation, personalisation and scalability are expected to play a critical role in shaping future telecommunications services.
The broader takeaway from the discussion was that connectivity alone is no longer sufficient as a differentiator. Instead, future growth is likely to be driven by meaningful digital experiences that seamlessly fit into consumers' daily routines, a direction that Tune Talk appears intent on pursuing through its "Kasi Tune" initiative.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tun Mahathir shares advice at 101: Eat to live, not live to eat [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former prime minister Tun Dr Mahathir Mohamad shared advice on health and longevity after celebrating his 101st birthday.
"We have to eat, but we have to eat because we want to live. Eat to live, not live to eat," he said in a brief video posted on his official Facebook page.
Reflecting on his well-being, Dr Mahathir admitted that while his appetite was no longer what it used to be, he continued to maintain his health.
"Yes, I'm like an ordinary person. Sometimes it's healthy, but sometimes it hurts a little. But when I went to the hospital, they gave me good medicine, and I continued to work."
When asked by moderator Shamsul Akmar how he truly felt, Dr Mahathir said he felt just like any other person who has grown old.
On July 10, Dr Mahathir thanked Malaysians for the overwhelming outpouring of birthday wishes and greetings.
He also posted a brief video on his Facebook page expressing his appreciation to well-wishers for their prayers and messages.
Accompanied by his wife, Tun Dr Siti Hasmah Mohd Ali, the couple conveyed their gratitude to those who remembered the occasion.
"Thank you very much for all your birthday wishes, prayers and greeting cards," Dr Mahathir said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: S.Korea court sentences ex-president to 2 years in jail in political funding case
Article:
SEOUL: A South Korean court sentenced former President Yoon Suk Yeol to two years in prison on Monday after finding him guilty of illegally receiving opinion polling services worth 270 million won (US$179,800) for free from a political broker, local media said.
The Seoul Central District Court found Yoon violated political funding laws by receiving 14 rounds of polling from the political broker at no cost and later exercising influence over the nomination of a former lawmaker to repay him, according to the ruling.
Yoon had denied the charges, saying he had not requested the polls or promised anything in return.
The decision differed from earlier court rulings involving former first lady Kim Keon Hee, which had found there was no quid pro quo in connection with the polling services.
Monday's ruling can be appealed.
Yoon, 65, is involved in eight legal cases. He is currently appealing a life sentence handed down in February after a court found him guilty of masterminding an insurrection tied to his short-lived declaration of martial law in 2024.
Other cases include a Supreme Court ruling last week that finalised a seven-year prison sentence for obstructing authorities' attempt to arrest him.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Federal Court upholds finding of possible pressure on witness in Syed Saddiq trial
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Federal Court today upheld the Court of Appeal's finding that there was evidence that a key prosecution witness in Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman's trial may have been subjected to improper pressure during the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission's (MACC) investigation.
Justice Datuk Che Mohd Ruzima Ghazali, delivering the majority judgment, rejected the prosecution's contention that the Court of Appeal had made "wild accusations" against the prosecution and the MACC when it questioned the credibility of prosecution witness Rafiq Hakim Razali.
Instead, Ruzima said the appellate court's findings were firmly grounded in evidence adduced during the trial.
Rafiq was the assistant treasurer of Armada and owner of Armada Bumi Bersatu Enterprise (ABBE), from whose Maybank account Syed Saddiq was charged with dishonestly misappropriating RM120,000.
"Upon perusing the relevant paragraphs of the Court of Appeal's judgment, I am of the view that the court referred to, analysed and dealt with the material evidence before making its finding that there might be truth in the defence's contention concerning Rafiq's testimony against the respondent," he said.
He said the Court of Appeal was entitled to infer that Rafiq's evidence may have been influenced after he was subjected to improper pressure during the investigation.
Ruzima also dismissed the prosecution's argument that the appellate court had unfairly criticised the MACC.
"I disagree with the prosecution's contention that the Court of Appeal made wild accusations against the prosecution and the MACC.
"The finding was based on evidence before the court and cannot be regarded as a wild accusation," he said.
He stressed that the administration of criminal justice required courts to guard against involuntary or improperly influenced evidence.
"No person should be compelled to confess to an offence or incriminate himself," he said.
Ruzima also noted that although the prosecution had submitted that Rafiq had committed criminal breach of trust, he was never charged with the offence.
The judge reiterated that every accused person is presumed innocent until proven guilty beyond reasonable doubt.
"However strong and grave the suspicion against an accused person may be, it can never take the place of proof."
Justice Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah concurred with Ruzima's findings, agreeing that the Court of Appeal had not erred in questioning the reliability of the witness's testimony.
The majority ruling formed part of the Federal Court's decision to dismiss the prosecution's final appeal and affirm Syed Saddiq's acquittal on one charge of abetting criminal breach of trust involving RM1 million in Armada funds, one charge of dishonest misappropriation of RM120,000, and two counts of money laundering.
Court of Appeal president Datuk Seri Abu Bakar Jais delivered a dissenting judgment, taking a different view on the dishonest misappropriation and money laundering charges, but agreeing that the abetment charge could not stand.
The appeal was dismissed by a 2-1 majority.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Student held over alleged stalking, harassment of university mate
Article:
SHAH ALAM: Police have arrested a public university student for allegedly harassing and stalking a female university mate since April.
The suspect, in his 20s, drew attention online after a closed-circuit television (CCTV) camera recording of him taking and sniffing a pair of shoes from an apartment unit went viral on social media.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Sarudin Samah said the suspect was arrested in Seksyen 7 yesterday, following a report lodged by the 22-year-old victim at 3.55pm.
He said the victim reported being repeatedly harassed and followed by the same man seen in the viral CCTV footage.
Shah Alam police chief Assistant Commissioner Sarudin Samah said the suspect, a man in his 20s, was arrested at about 8.30pm yesterday in Section 7, hours after the victim lodged a police report at 3.55pm.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Why the Federal Court upheld Syed Saddiq's acquittal
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Federal Court upheld Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman's acquittal after ruling that the prosecution failed to prove the essential ingredients of the offences and did not establish any appealable error in the Court of Appeal's findings.
In the majority judgment, Justices Datuk Che Mohd Ruzima Ghazali and Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah found that the High Court's convictions on all four charges were unsafe, dismissing the prosecution's final appeal.
On the charge of abetting criminal breach of trust (CBT) involving RM1 million in Bersatu Youth funds, the majority agreed that the prosecution failed to prove the principal offence allegedly committed by the wing's assistant treasurer Rafiq Hakim Razali.
Ruzima held that the lower court was correct in finding that merely withdrawing the RM1 million did not amount to criminal breach of trust under Section 405 of the Penal Code.
He said Bersatu's constitution prohibited expenditure exceeding RM500,000 without prior approval from the party's supreme council, but did not prohibit the withdrawal of funds.
"The Bersatu constitution is not law. It is merely a set of rules governing members of the political party," he said, adding that a breach of the party's rules did not automatically amount to a criminal offence.
He further held that as the prosecution failed to establish that the principal offender had committed CBT, the charge that the Muar member of Parliament had abetted the offence could not stand.
On the second charge of dishonest misappropriation involving RM120,000, Ruzima said the prosecution failed to prove one of the essential elements of the offence — that the money belonged to someone other than Syed Saddiq.
The judge noted that prosecution witnesses themselves testified that the money was raised specifically for Syed Saddiq's campaign for the 14th General Election and was deposited into Armada Bumi Bersatu Enterprise's (ABBE) account only because its owner, Rafiq, had offered to use the account to receive the donations.
He said there was "not an iota of evidence" showing the RM120,000 belonged to ABBE, Rafiq Hakim or any other person.
"Based on the evidence adduced by the prosecution, the most preferable inference is that the RM120,000 does not belong to ABBE, Rafiq, or someone else, but to Syed Saddiq himself."
Although the money originally came from donors, Ruzima said ownership changed once the donations were given to finance Syed Saddiq's election campaign, making him the beneficial owner of the funds.
The majority also rejected the prosecution's challenge to the Court of Appeal's observations concerning the testimony of key prosecution witness Rafiq.
Ruzima held that the appellate court was entitled to conclude there was evidence supporting the defence's contention that Rafiq may have tailored his testimony after being subjected to improper pressure during the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission's investigation (MACC).
The judge rejected the prosecution's argument that the Court of Appeal had made 'wild accusations' against the MACC, saying the appellate court's findings were grounded in the evidence before it.
As the two money laundering charges were predicated on the alleged dishonest misappropriation, the majority held that those charges also could not be sustained once the dishonest misappropriation of property offence failed.
Sequerah concurred with Ruzima's reasoning, agreeing that the prosecution had failed to demonstrate any appealable error in the Court of Appeal's judgment.
The majority therefore affirmed the appellate court's decision acquitting Syed Saddiq on all four charges, bringing to an end the prosecution's final appeal.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Melaka police detain businessman over suspected involvement in 10 child sexual assault cases
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — Police have detained a local businessman believed to be involved in 10 cases of sexual assault against boys between March and yesterday.
Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the 36-year-old man was detained by members of the public at the Taman Desa Duyong community hall in Melaka at about 6pm yesterday before being handed over to police, according to national news agency Bernama.
He said the 10 victims were aged between eight and 13, with the alleged incidents said to have taken place at playgrounds, surau, supermarkets, eateries and the suspect’s house.
Police said the suspect allegedly approached the victims at various locations before carrying out acts that are being investigated under the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017.

Title: Malaysia, South Korea team up to train 200 AI and digital professionals
Article:
SHAH ALAM, July 13 — The Ministry of Digital today launched the Korea-Asean Digital Academy (KADA), a joint initiative with the Republic of Korea to develop industry-ready artificial intelligence (AI) and digital professionals in Malaysia.
Coordinated by the Malaysia Digital Economy Corporation (MDEC), the programme will provide specialised training in AI and emerging digital technologies through four cohorts from 2026 to 2027, with a target of producing about 200 industry-ready Malaysian AI and digital professionals.
“Through the programme, participants will undergo specialised training in AI and emerging digital technologies, equipping them with practical skills, global perspectives and industry competencies to meet the evolving demands of the digital economy.
“Beyond talent development, KADA strengthens cooperation between Malaysia, the Republic of Korea and Asean member states by promoting knowledge exchange and advancing AI capability development across the region,” MDEC said in a statement today.
The programme was launched by MDEC chief executive officer Anuar Fariz Fadzil, Republic of Korea Ambassador to Malaysia Yeo Seung-bae, National IT Industry Promotion Agency (NIPA) executive vice president Kim Deuk-jung, Universiti Teknologi MARA (UiTM) deputy vice-chancellor (Academic and International) Professor Dr Yamin Yasin, and UiTM Faculty of Computer and Mathematical Sciences dean Professor Dr Norjansalika Janom at UiTM.
Malaysia was selected as one of the participating countries under KADA following its endorsement at the 6th Asean Digital Ministers’ Meeting (6th ADGMIN) in January, reflecting international confidence in the country’s digital ecosystem, institutional capabilities and commitment to developing future-ready digital talent.
MDEC is implementing the programme in collaboration with strategic partners from the government, academia and industry.
UiTM, through its Faculty of Computer and Mathematical Sciences, was selected as Malaysia’s host institution following an evaluation involving selected Premier Digital Tech Institutions.
The programme will be delivered by ABLEARN, NIPA’s appointed service provider, in collaboration with Garranto Academy as the local operational partner. — Bernama

Title: Alor Setar premise accused of being foreign-run found operated by Malaysians, says council
Article:
ALOR SETAR, July 13 — The Alor Setar City Council (MBAS) today denied claims that a premise on Jalan Langgar was operated by foreigners, as alleged in a video that went viral on social media recently.
Alor Setar Mayor Datuk Abdul Gafar Yahya said following the allegation, an inspection was carried out on July 9 by the MBAS Disease Control, Health Promotion and Environmental Health Inspectorate Division, Licensing Division and Enforcement Division, together with the Kota Setar District Health Office (PKD).
“During the inspection, the workers at the premise as well as those manning the front counter were Malaysian citizens. Therefore, the Kota Setar PKD did not take any action against the premise as there was no violation or offence under its jurisdiction.
“Checks also found that the premise has a valid business licence issued by MBAS,” he said in a statement today.
He said the only offence identified by the council was the placement of business goods in front of the premise by the owner without obtaining a permit from MBAS.
“The offence falls under Paragraph 25(2) of the Food Establishment Licensing By-Laws (MPKS) 1989, which prohibits the placement of business goods on the pavement without a permit.
“MBAS remains committed to carrying out inspections and enforcement fairly and in accordance with the provisions of the law. The public is advised not to spread unverified information and to always obtain information from official sources,” he said.
Earlier, a video went viral showing a grocery shop in the city centre that was allegedly operated by members of the Rohingya ethnic group.
The person in the video had called on MBAS to provide an explanation, claiming that the council had failed to take action on the matter. — Bernama

Title: Malaysia confident of 4-5pc growth in 2026 despite global headwinds, says economy minister
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — The government remains bullish that Malaysia’s economy could grow between four to five per cent despite the International Monetary Fund (IMF) lowering its global economic growth projection to three per cent, Economy Minister Akmal Nasir Nasrullah said today.
The optimism stems from Malaysia’s strong economic showing, which grew by 5.4 per cent in the first quarter of 2026 as well as the ringgit’s improving strength and inflation that remains steady at two per cent since May.
Akmal Nasir also highlighted Malaysia’s robust trade performance as the country’s total trade value jumped by 18.3 per cent to RM1.5 trillion between January and May this year.
“Exports burgeoned by 24.3 per cent to RM793.8 billion while imports climbed by 11.8 per cent to RM661.1 billion, with a trade surplus of RM132.8 billion.
“The Leading Index increased by 1.3 per cent in April while the Industrial Production Index for the manufacturinf sector expanded by 6.4 per cent between January and May, especially involving the electric and electronix products.
“The Manufacturing PMI rose to 50.7 points in June, signalling that manufacturing activities have bounced back,” Akmal Nasir said during an online briefing after the National Economic Action Council (MTEN) meeting today.
Akmal Nasir added that the ringgit has also strengthened by 8.3 per cent against the US dollar since 2025-end, as of July 3, and that Bank Negara has maintained the Overnight Rate Policy (OPR) at 2.75 per cent.
He said the government will also reduce unnecessary structural and compliance costs, expand market access to attract quality investments and expedite adoption of technology besides ensuring sufficient fuel, food and industrial input supplies.

Title: TVET graduates to get job offers before graduation through industry links, says Zahid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — The government has assured that graduates of Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institutions will enjoy a high employability rate through strategic collaboration with industry, enabling them to receive job offers before completing their studies.
Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the approach was implemented through early engagement between TVET institutions and companies as well as industry players to ensure the courses offered were aligned with labour market needs.
“What we do is engage with companies and industry players beforehand to ensure the courses offered by TVET institutions are relevant to the jobs and industry requirements.
“That is why the graduate employability rate is 100 per cent because job offers are made before they complete their courses. This means there is no issue of course mismatch, minimum wage concerns or the absence of job offers,” said Ahmad Zahid, who is also Rural and Regional Development Minister and chairman of the National TVET Council.
He told reporters this after delivering his address to the TVET@KKDW (Ministry of Rural and Regional Development) contingent for WorldSkills Shanghai 2026 here today. Also present was Deputy Rural and Regional Development Minister Datuk Rubiah Wang.
Ahmad Zahid said the government was also focusing on implementing High TVET courses to ensure graduates possessed skills that met industry demands.
Earlier, in his speech, Ahmad Zahid expressed confidence that the TVET@KKDW contingent had the potential to make history at the WorldSkills Shanghai 2026 competition.
“We want our participants to demonstrate strong mental resilience and emotional fortitude to prove that Malaysian competitors can excel on the international stage,” he said.
He also reminded the participants to continue enhancing their skills, strengthen their discipline and remain focused during the remaining two months of preparations before the competition, which will be held in Shanghai, China, from September 22 to 27.
The TVET@KKDW contingent comprises nine participants from MARA TVET, Universiti Kuala Lumpur (UniKL) and GIATMARA, who will compete in nine skill categories, including Fashion Technology, Cyber Security and Electronics. — Bernama

Title: NRD approves most MyKAS applications, over 1,000 citizenship cases still pending
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — The National Registration Department (NRD) approved 286 MyKAS applications, or 96 per cent of the 298 applications received for the document between 2022 and May 31, 2026, Deputy Home Minister Datuk Seri Dr Shamsul Anuar Nasarah said.
MyKAS (Kad Pengenalan Pemastautin Sementara) is a green, temporary resident identity card issued by the NRD to non-citizens.
He said the department also received 3,117 late birth registration applications from the Indian community, of which 2,810 applications, or 90.1 per cent, had been approved, while another 251 were still being processed.
“For citizenship applications, a total of 1,018 applications have been recorded. Of these, 503 applications, or 49.4 per cent, are still being processed, while 141 applications, or 13.9 per cent, have been approved,” he said during the Special Chamber sitting in the Dewan Rakyat today in reply to a question from Dr V. Sivakumar (PH-Batu Gajah) on the status of documentation and identity card ownership among the Indian community in Malaysia.
On citizenship approvals, Shamsul Anuar explained that the approval statistics referred to applications that had received a decision, with citizenship certificates issued and handed over to the applicants.
“Although an application may have been approved by the Home Ministry (KDN), it will still be recorded as being processed in the NRD system if the certificate has yet to be applied for, printed or handed over to the applicant,” he said.
To address documentation issues in rural areas, Shamsul Anuar said the NRD had adopted a proactive approach through the Menyemai Kasih Rakyat (MEKAR) programme by deploying officers to the ground to ensure those facing access constraints were not left behind in obtaining identity documents.
He also stressed that the ministry had not appointed any non-governmental organisations (NGOs) as intermediaries for NRD applications, as all processes are governed by legal provisions.
Replying to a supplementary question from Sivakumar on the main causes of late birth registration, Shamsul Anuar said the NRD had identified several contributing factors, including parents’ lack of awareness of the requirement to register a child’s birth within the stipulated period of 60 days in Peninsular Malaysia and 42 days in Sabah and Sarawak.
Other factors included family-related issues such as separation or divorce, financial constraints preventing parents from visiting registration offices, and incomplete supporting documents.
To expedite approvals for late birth registration applications, he said the NRD had delegated approval powers to the state level, enabling applications to be processed more quickly without requiring every case to be referred to the headquarters.
“This delegation of authority has also reduced bureaucratic burdens, shortened application processing times and improved the efficiency of service delivery to the public,” he said. — Bernama

Title: New Admiralty Jurisdiction Bill aims to overhaul Malaysia’s maritime dispute framework
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — The Admiralty Jurisdiction Bill 2026, which seeks to govern maritime matters and disputes within the shipping industry, has been referred to the Dewan Rakyat Special Select Committee for further scrutiny.
Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said moved the motion after the bill’s first reading, allowing all relevant stakeholders to examine it.
The motion was subsequently passed by the Dewan Rakyat on a majority voice vote.
